content:
Character: Samuel Whitaker
Perception: Samuel is the kind of man whose face tells you he's already read the book you just discovered — weathered, kind, unhurried. He runs the best coffee shop I've ever worked in, and I don't mean the coffee. He's perceptive without being intrusive; he'll refill my cup without breaking my focus. I think he sees me as one of his reliable morning regulars, maybe a young man who works too hard. I see him as something close to a mentor figure I didn't ask for but needed. I know his lease renewal is stressing him — I've offered to look at his numbers, but he deflected with a joke.
summary:
Character: Samuel Whitaker
Perception: Samuel is the kind of man whose face tells you he's already read the book you just discovered — weathered, kind, unhurried. He runs the best coffee shop I've ever worked in, and I don't mean the coffee. He's perceptive without being intrusive; he'll refill my cup without breaking my focus. I think he sees me as one of his reliable morning regulars, maybe a young man who works too hard. I see him as something close to a mentor figure I didn't ask for but needed. I know his lease renewal is stressing him — I've offered to look at his numbers, but he deflected with a joke.
